name: Mounds Macaroons
recipeIngredient: 1 package Cream cheese (3 oz) - at room temperature
1/4 cup Unsalted butter (1/2 stick) - at room temperature
1 1/2 cups Mounds bars (1/2-inch pieces - about 7 1/2 oz)
recipeInstructions:
Since this is a firm dough, the macaroons will hold their shape during baking and produce large mound-shaped cookies. This is appropriate since they contain pieces of Mounds candy bars. Preheat oven to 275°F. Line two cookie sheets with parchment paper.
Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl.
Using an electric mixer, beat sugar, cream cheese, and butter in a large bowl until fluffy.
Beat in egg, vanilla, almond extract, and orange peel.
Add flour mixture and mix just until combined.
Stir in 2 3/4 cups coconut. Add Mounds pieces and stir until combined.
Spread 1 1/4 cups coconut on a plate. Using two tablespoons of dough for each cookie, roll the dough into 1 1/2-inch rounds. Roll each round in coconut. Arrange rounds 2 inches apart on prepared cookie sheets.
Bake cookies until they are puffed and the coconut is light golden (about 35 minutes). Cool on cookie sheets for 5 minutes. Transfer to racks; cool completely.
Stir chocolate in the top of a double boiler set over simmering water until melted and smooth. Remove from heat. Dip a fork into melted chocolate and drizzle lines of chocolate across tops of cookies. Refrigerate cookies until chocolate sets (about 30 minutes). (Can be prepared up to 3 days ahead. Refrigerate in airtight containers.) Serve cold or at room temperature.
Makes about 20.
